entities.name: SOIL EROSION
entities.description: The wearing away of topsoil by physical forces, identified as a major constraint in north-eastern India

entities.name: SHIFTING CULTIVATION
entities.description: An agricultural system of clearing land and moving, which contributes to soil erosion

entities.name: NORTH-EASTERN INDIA
entities.description: The geographic region where the study on soil erosion and productivity was conducted

entities.name: TERRACING
entities.description: A cost-intensive soil conservation technology involving the creation of steps on slopes

entities.name: WEED COVER
entities.description: The use of managed weed growth to protect the soil surface and minimize erosion

entities.name: SOIL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The capacity of the soil to support plant growth and yield

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: Zea mays, a cereal crop used in the field experiments

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogea, a legume crop used in the field experiments

entities.name: CONTOUR LINES
entities.description: The practice of planting across the slope to reduce runoff and erosion

entities.name: NATURAL VEGETATION
entities.description: Native plant species used as buffer strips to control runoff

entities.name: BUFFER STRIPS
entities.description: Strips of vegetation used to reduce sediment runoff and soil erosion

entities.name: SEDIMENT CONCENTRATION
entities.description: The amount of soil particles present in runoff water

entities.name: SOIL LOSS
entities.description: The quantity of soil removed from the land due to erosion

entities.name: SOC
entities.description: Soil Organic Carbon content lost during soil erosion

entities.name: AVAILABLE N
entities.description: Available Nitrogen in the soil lost during soil erosion

entities.name: AVAILABLE P
entities.description: Available Phosphorus in the soil lost during soil erosion

entities.name: WEED STRIPS
entities.description: Managed strips of weeds used to control erosion and form mini-terraces

entities.name: WEED MULCH
entities.description: A layer of weed material on the soil surface to reduce erosion

entities.name: MINI-TERRACES
entities.description: Small terrace-like structures formed by weed management that promote plant growth

entities.name: PLANT AND ROOT GROWTH
entities.description: The development and expansion of plant biomass and root systems

entities.name: SELECTIVE WEED RETENTION
entities.description: The practice of keeping specific weeds to reduce erosion and modify land slope

entities.name: LAND SLOPE
entities.description: The gradient of the terrain, which influences the rate of soil erosion
